core_model.structure_summary: A coupled system of first-order ODEs in which the reaction/production rates are supplied by a learned nonlinear surrogate function, with each state subject to a state-proportional growth-plus-decay (dilution) loss term and driven by external control inputs.
core_model.canonical_form: \dot{z} = b(t)\,q_z\!\left(z,e;\theta\right),\qquad \dot{e} = q_e\!\left(u;\theta\right) - \left(\mu(z,e)\,\mathbf{1} + d(e)\right)\circ e,\qquad q_z = f_{\mathrm{NN}}(e)\,h(z),\qquad z(0)=z_0,\ e(0)=e_0

variables.meaning: extensive/concentration-like quantities and internal expression-level variables
externally manipulable input driving production rates
kinetic parameters and neural-surrogate weights encoding the embedded network constraints

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A coupled system of first-order ODEs with externally driven production terms and state-proportional loss (dilution/decay) terms, with the surrogate reduced to a generic nonlinear rate function.

notes: Two candidate central models: (1) the hybrid physics-informed dynamic ODE system (chosen here, matches the abstract's stated framework), and (2) the embedded flux-balance constrained optimization (max F s.t. S v = 0, bounds) plus downstream MPC/MHE optimizations. The neural-network surrogate replaces the inner constrained optimization, so constrained_optimization is retained as a secondary feature of the core but dropped from the pure dynamical skeleton.
